inlineContent.text: Learn how to add an OpenAPI document, generate server stubs using the OpenAPI plugin, and integrate the generated code into the app.
Create a new OpenAPI document to define the service’s HTTP API.
Add the OpenAPI dependencies noted on the right.
directory create a base OpenAPI document named
The document should include a version,
providing a high level summary of the API, and
that is used in generated documentation for the API as illustrated on the right.
In the openapi.yaml add a schema for a TODO item, mapping to the model defined in
property constrains OpenAPI to verify the string format matches a UUID.
defines a list of TODO items, referencing the schema for
the schemas to support a list of TODOs
the schemas to support creating a new
get route to return a list of the TODOs.
post route to create a new TODO
a route to create a new TODO.
The parameter for the route uses a reference to a component, which allows for re-use.
routes to retrieve and to delete an individual TODO.
The TODO identifier will be used to determine which TOTO to retrieve or delete.
the parameter for the TODO identifier.
Configure and use the OpenAPI plugin to generate code from the OpenAPI document.
This provides configuration for the generator to create both types from the schema and server stubs.
Add the OpenAPI generator plugin to the plugins section as illistrated on the right.
Open a terminal, stop the running server with ctrl+C, and rebuild the project.
Remember to use the –product switch to speed up the build.
Whenever you build your package, if the OpenAPI document or the config file has changed, the plugin regenerates the Swift code behind the scenes.
to provide the implementation for the stubs.
is a protocol generated from the OpenAPI document and defines the methods to implement the server.

to register your implementation, enabling your app to use the transport for routes you implemented.
Add the code on the right to use the implementation you created.

abstract.text: Ensure you have the right tools and example code before learning how to write your Swift server application.

doc://com.apple.ASE.getting-started/tuto.title: Clone and build the tutorial repository

abstract.text: Ensure you have the right tools and example code before learning how to write your Swift server application.

The tutorial example contains a Package.swift file that defines the dependencies and targets for the app, as well as a Sources directory with starter files. The finished tutorial is also provided in the Completed folder.
